sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: A class is a blueprint for creating objects. An object is an instance of that blueprint.

sections.heading: Class Declaration
sections.content: The most basic class consists of the class keyword followed by a name.

sections.heading: Creating an Object
sections.content: To create an instance, call the constructor like a function. Kotlin does not use the new keyword.

sections.heading: Primary Constructor
sections.content: The primary constructor is declared in the class header. It initializes properties directly.

sections.heading: Initializer Block (init)
sections.content: The primary constructor cannot contain logic. Use the init block for initialization code.

sections.heading: Properties: Getters and Setters
sections.content: When you declare a property, Kotlin automatically generates getters and setters. val generates only a getter, while var generates both.

rows.description: Visible in the class and its subclasses.
Visible only within the same module (project).

sections.heading: Constructors with Default Values
sections.content: Constructors can have default parameter values, eliminating the need for multiple overloaded constructors.

sections.heading: Abstract Classes
sections.content: An abstract class cannot be instantiated directly. It is meant to be inherited by other classes.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Use class to define a blueprint.
Primary constructors are defined in the class header.
Use init blocks for initialization logic.
Properties (val / var) automatically generate getters and setters.
No new keyword is required to create objects.
